name: Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park Loop
name_original: Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Hornuez y Embalse de Linares — circular desde Maderuelo por el Parque Natural de las Hoces del Río Riaza
description: This moderate 33.0-mile mountain biking loop through Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park offers dramatic gorges and vulture sightings.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Maderuelo offers diverse terrain, from routes alongside the Linares del Arroyo Reservoir to trails within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park. The landscape features impressive gorges, varied rock formations, and a mix of hills and valleys covered in juniper and holm oak forests. This region provides a range of physical challenges and scenic beauty for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Maderuelo The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Ayllón, Puerto del Infante y El Muyo — circular desde Ayllón, a 39.9 miles (64.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates varied terrain through a mountain atmosphere with significant ascents. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park Loop, a moderate 33.0 miles (53.1 km) path. This route explores the natural park, featuring impressive gorges and diverse rock formations. Local mountain bikers also love the Valdevacas de Montejo – Riaza River Gorge loop from Maderuelo, a 26.0 miles (42.0 km) trail leading through the Riaza River Gorge, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Maderuelo is defined by the scenic Linares del Arroyo Reservoir, the rugged Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, and trails through juniper and holm oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Maderuelo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Maderuelo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Maderuelo?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Maderuelo, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find routes exploring the Linares del Arroyo Reservoir, the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, and the surrounding juniper and holm oak forests.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Maderuelo?
A: The trails around Maderuelo cater to various abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 28 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced riders can find suitable challenges.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes near Maderuelo?
A: Yes, Maderuelo offers 2 easy mountain bike routes that are suitable for families. These trails typically feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outing with children. Look for routes with gentler gradients and well-maintained paths.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Maderuelo?
A: Many trails around Maderuelo are dog-friendly, especially those outside the most sensitive areas of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural park areas to protect wildlife and ensure their safety. Always check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Maderuelo?
A: Mountain biking in Maderuelo offers diverse scenery. You'll ride alongside the scenic Linares del Arroyo Reservoir, through impressive gorges and past spectacular rock formations in the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, and navigate hills and valleys covered in juniper and holm oak forests. The region provides a rich blend of natural beauty and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Maderuelo area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Maderuelo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080377487/hoces-del-rio-riaza-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17505668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdevacas de Montejo – Riaza River Gorge loop from Maderuelo&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several attractions. Along the trails, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2557040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct of the Madrid–Burgos Railway over the Riaza Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5327014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of the Hoces del Río Riaza&lt;/a&gt;. The historic village of Maderuelo itself is a highlight, with its medieval charm. Nearby, you can visit the medieval village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Langa de Duero&lt;/a&gt; or the Templar castle in &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo de Robledo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Maderuelo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Maderuelo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the Linares del Arroyo Reservoir, and the challenging yet rewarding paths through the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park.

Q: Are there any specific restrictions for cycling in the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park?
A: The Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park is a protected area known for its rich flora and fauna, especially birds of prey. While mountain biking is generally permitted on designated trails, it's crucial to stay on marked paths to minimize disturbance to wildlife, particularly during nesting seasons. Always respect local signage and regulations within the park.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Maderuelo?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking in Maderuelo. During these seasons, the weather is typically milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Maderuelo?
A: Maderuelo, being a popular destination, offers various parking options within or near the village. Look for designated parking areas, especially near the entrance to the village or close to trailheads. Some routes might also have specific parking spots indicated on their komoot tour pages.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Maderuelo provide excellent viewpoints, especially those that traverse the hills overlooking the Linares del Arroyo Reservoir or the gorges of the Riaza River. The 'mountain atmosphere' of the region ensures plenty of elevated spots for panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes and rock formations.
tips: Rolling through the Linares del Arroyo reservoir we have a beautiful panoramic view of the reservoir island.

Surrounded by an extensive juniper forest and rolling close to the Hoces del Riaza, we come across the beautiful Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Hornuez. A place in the middle of nature that transmits peace and tranquility.
Demanding "single track" that advances through the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, a wonder for mountain bike lovers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop is rated as moderate. While the broader Riaza region offers trails ranging from easy to very challenging, this specific route provides a balanced experience suitable for riders with some experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for this loop starts and ends near Maderuelo, suggesting that parking would be available in or around the village. It's always a good idea to check local signage for designated parking areas before your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain characteristic of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park. Expect paths through impressive gorges and canyons carved by the Riaza River, potentially alongside sessile juniper forests. While the Komoot tour is moderate, some sections in the wider park can be technical, so be prepared for a mix of surfaces.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see while mountain biking this trail?
faqs.answer: The park is a vital sanctuary for wildlife, most famously for its large colony of over 600 pairs of griffon vultures. You'll likely see these magnificent raptors soaring overhead, along with other birds of prey and steppe species. Keep an eye out for other local fauna as you ride through the natural landscape.

faqs.question: Is the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Natural Park generally welcomes visitors, specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary. It's best to keep your dog on a leash to protect the abundant wildlife, especially the ground-nesting birds and other animals in this important sanctuary. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, especially in the gorges, so an early start is recommended. Winter rides are possible but be prepared for colder temperatures and potentially wet or muddy conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As a natural park, there are typically no entrance fees to access the Hoces del Río Riaza. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and wildlife. No specific permits are generally required for mountain biking on established trails, but it's good practice to check for any temporary restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks will I pass on the route?
faqs.answer: This loop offers several points of interest. You'll get a View of the Island in Linares del Arroyo Reservoir, and pass by the historic Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hornuez. The route also includes sections of the Hoces del Río Riaza Trail, offering stunning views of the gorges.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike loop intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will ride along parts of the Senda del Río, Senda entre Puentes, Senda de la Ermita del Casuar, and the Embalse de Linares del Arroyo. It also uses portions of Camino de Casuar, Camino de los Valles, and Camino a Maderuelo, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: The loop starts near Maderuelo, a charming village where you can likely find local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Exploring these nearby towns is a great way to experience the local culture and refuel after your ride.

faqs.question: What makes mountain biking in Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park unique?
faqs.answer: The unique appeal of mountain biking here lies in the combination of its dramatic geological features—deep gorges and canyons—and its status as a crucial raptor sanctuary. Riding through this landscape while potentially observing hundreds of griffon vultures soaring above offers a truly immersive and awe-inspiring natural experience.
